review.summary: The change replaces public dispatch metadata (pilot, PR, Head SHA, reservation run, request ID) with a single opaque numeric ticket, recovering private details only inside the protected environment from a bounded reservation artifact. Ticket resolution validates run identity, window, artifact identity, lease binding, GitHub snapshot, and budget counters. Replay is prevented by requiring exactly one matching opaque-titled run in the ledger and rejecting non-opaque run metadata. Token is stripped on artifact redirects and downloads are size-bounded. Tests exercise each path and assert on error messages. No open P0/P1/P2 findings.

findings.finding: api_bytes bounds body to maximum_bytes (100KB) and parse_reservation_archive caps the member at 50KB, but a maliciously crafted zip could still expand within limits; mitigated by single-member name check and flag_bits encryption rejection, leaving only bounded residual parsing risk.
validate_ticket rejects bool and non-positive ticket IDs (isinstance bool guard), preventing True from passing as 1; tested by test_active_ticket_requires_positive_integer.

verify_run_budget now requires exactly one run matching 'ticket N' title and rejects any non-opaque run name, enforcing single-use tickets; proven by test_reused_opaque_ticket_is_denied and test_public_ledger_rejects_non_opaque_run_name.
_SafeRedirectHandler removes Authorization header and forbids non-HTTPS redirects on artifact download, preventing token leak to redirect targets.

Config flag renamed public_dispatch_metadata_accepted to opaque_dispatch_verified and defaults false; SECURITY.md item 8 requires inspecting executor SHA and inputs before setting true, preserving fail-closed activation.

verification: Public workflow inputs reduced to a single numeric ticket_id; run-name and env expose no pilot/PR/head/request, asserted by StaticWorkflowTests.
resolve_dispatch_ticket verifies live in_progress reservation run identity, approval window, single live artifact, title/artifact identity, and open-PR resolution before dispatch.
validate_reservation_context binds lease, policy SHA, GitHub snapshot, and family/pilot budgets to the opaque ticket; over-budget pilot denied in tests.
verify_run_budget enforces single-use opaque ticket and rejects non-opaque ledger titles.
Artifact download strips token on redirect and enforces 100KB/50KB size caps.
publish path unchanged in effect: still binds body marker, Verdict count, and Head SHA before posting via review token.
requirement_coverage: Credential exposure: token stripped on redirect; artifacts fetched only from api.github.com.
Budget bypass: per-pilot and total counters enforced from private ledger plus single-use ticket check.
Fail-closed: opaque_dispatch_verified defaults false; malformed/expired/duplicate reservation denies.
Unsafe automation: executor cannot merge/push; publish only posts schema-bound comment.
Metadata disclosure: public dispatch exposes only opaque ticket, tested statically.
residual_risk: Correctness of the opaque-ticket model depends on the unseen u1-model-reservation workflow producing exactly one non-expired artifact and unique run titles; not provable from this diff.
README claim that global concurrency prevents two runs consuming one ticket relies on GitHub concurrency semantics not verifiable here, though single-use ledger check adds defense in depth.
